strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 defines webhookBodyParser via express.raw(...) and passes it only to app.post('/webhook', webhookBodyParser, ...), keeping raw-body capture and JSON-parse-failure handling scoped to the webhook route, whereas P1 installs a generic app.use((err, req, res, next) => {...}) error handler right after the global express.json(), making its 401-vs-400 signature branching apply to JSON-parse errors on any route in the app.

P1 loads the secret with fs.readFileSync(...).trim(), guarding against a trailing newline in data/webhook-secret.txt breaking HMAC comparisons against the real provider, while P2's identical readFileSync call omits .trim(), so any trailing newline in that file would be baked into every computed signature; P1 also ships 9 new webhook tests (including malformed-JSON and malformed-signature-format cases) versus P2's 3.

P2's JSON.parse/validation logic lives entirely inside the /webhook handler so future POST routes are unaffected, whereas P1's app.use((err, req, res, next) => {...}) sits ahead of all routes and would apply webhook's invalid_signature/invalid_body semantics to any future route's malformed JSON body.

P2 factors body-shape validation into a standalone named function isValidWebhookBody(body) that can be read and reused independently, while P1 inlines the equivalent multi-field check as one large if condition directly inside the app.post('/webhook', ...) handler.
